action.reasoning: Observation: eval now fails inside GraphConv einsum with x interpreted as having node dimension 12 instead of 207, which means the model is receiving input shaped [B, 12, 207] while GraphConv expects [B, C, N, T]. Analysis: this is a forward-layout bug in predict/model compatibility, not a checkpoint-quality issue. The safest high-value move is to make the model forward explicitly accept [B,T,N], convert to [B,1,N,T] before all conv/graph ops, and keep output [B,T,N]. Also make end-conv variant tolerate optional horizon_scale/bias keys. Then locally smoke test predict.py on ema_weighted.pt and submit it immediately once loading/inference works. Decision: rewrite model.py cleanly with exact tensor layout handling and robust checkpoint inference, then run local predict smoke test and submit ema_weighted.pt.
